user_query:
You're my payment architecture advisor. Read input/requirements.json and input/pricing_assumptions.json. Based on our constraints, decide between PayRam, x402, or a hybrid (PayRam as the x402 settlement layer) and produce four artifacts under output/:

1) Write output/recommendation.json with exactly these keys:
- chosen_architecture (one of: "payram", "x402", "payram_as_x402_layer")
- reasoning.privacy (string)
- reasoning.kyc (string)
- reasoning.token_support (string)
- reasoning.facilitator_dependency (string)
- reasoning.settlement_finality (string)
- reasoning.http_native (string)
- supports_tokens (boolean)
- supports_chains (boolean)
- meets_latency (boolean)
- micropayments_viable (boolean)
- notes (array of strings)

2) Write output/decision_matrix.csv with columns: Criterion,PayRam,x402,Hybrid,Winner. Include at least these rows (Criterion values exactly):
- Privacy / Identity Isolation
- KYC / Facilitator Dependency
- Token Support (USDT/USDC/BTC)
- Chain Support (Tron/Base/Bitcoin)
- HTTP-Native Integration
- Settlement Finality
- Micropayments ($0.01)
- EU (MiCA) Compliance Control
Set "Winner" per row according to our needs.

3) Write output/architecture.md with these required sections and phrases:
- A top-level heading "# Overview"
- A section "## Payment Flow" describing a hybrid flow if chosen, and explicitly using the phrases "unique deposit address" and "on-chain confirmation"
- A section "## Privacy Considerations" that mentions HTTP metadata exposure risks
- A section "## Token & Chain Support" that explicitly mentions "USDT on Tron" and "BTC"
- A section "## Settlement & Latency" that references either "Tron" or "Base" as a low-latency option
- A section "## Fallback Plan"
- A section "## Why not pure x402" that references facilitator KYC and names at least one of "Stripe" or "Coinbase"

4) Write output/risk_register.json as an array of objects, each with id, title, severity, mitigation. Include at least these ids: "infrastructure_maintenance", "bitcoin_confirmation_delays", "x402_optimistic_settlement_risk", "eu_mica_uncertainty".

Ground every conclusion in the scenario and the public properties of the two systems (privacy, KYC/facilitator dependency, token and chain support, and settlement finality). Use only relative paths under output/ for created files.
